tossups.question: Description acceptable. Chef Francesco Longoni apparently invented a new type of sog-reducing pizza box for this project. The man who conceived this project last discussed it while watching Remember the Titans and was convinced by his son Reed that it originally looked too much like a giant penis. The Seele Group had to build the largest autoclave in the world to produce 45-foot tall pieces for this project which is also massively equipped with Pacific Chairs. The details for this project, heavily modeled on the (*) quad at Stanford, were filled in by the architects working under Norman Foster. A design studio in this replacement of 1 Infinite Loop is one of the few places where pods are sealed off from the rest of the environment and is overseen by Jony Ive. For 10 points, name this gigantic circular building in Cupertino where engineers are presumably designing new iPhones.
tossups.answer: Apple Park [or the new Apple headquarters; the Apple's spaceship; Apple's UFO; or anything involving Apple and some sort of new circular building where its employees work in what I can imagine is some form of dystopian future]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Apple Park [or the new Apple headquarters; the Apple's spaceship; Apple's UFO; or anything involving Apple and some sort of new circular building where its employees work in what I can imagine is some form of dystopian future]

The Seele Group had to build the largest autoclave in the world to produce 45-foot tall pieces for this project which is also massively equipped with Pacific Chairs. The details for this project, heavily modeled on the (*) quad at Stanford, were filled in by the architects working under Norman Foster. bonuses.leadin: Preventative “bolts” installed on buildings following one of these natural disasters have become decorative tourist attractions in Charleston, South Carolina. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these natural disasters. The construction of buildings on lead, steel, and rubber foundations, a technique known as base isolation, can reduce the risk of collapse from these disasters, which often occur on fault lines.

Taipei 101 contains the world’s largest example of this earthquake-mitigating measure, essentially a large, golden pendulum suspended from its 91st floor. This technology counters swaying motions common during earthquakes.
Norman Foster designed this spaceship-like building in Cupertino to rest on 692 steel saucers, making it the largest base-isolated structure in the world.
answers_sanitized: tuned mass damper [or TMD or seismic damper]
Apple Park [accept descriptions of the Apple headquarters]

bonuses.leadin: One of these locations on Boylston Street in Boston features a glass spiral staircase, while the one near the Burj Khalifa has windows made out of carbon fiber rods that open when temperature rises. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these typically sleek and minimalist retail locations that sell products such as the iPhone and the Macbook.

The Apple Park and many Apple stores were designed by an architectural firm named for this architect “plus Partners.” This architect also designed the Hearst Tower in Manhattan and the HSBC Tower in Hong Kong.

This former chief designer at Apple designed Apple Park along with Foster and Partners. He is credited with implementing the translucent plastic shell on the iMac G3 and the decision to move away from the skeuomorphic design for the interface of IOS 7.
answers: Norman Foster [or Norman Robert Foster, Baron Foster of Thames Bank]
answers_sanitized: Apple stores [prompt on electronics stores; reject "Apple Park"]
Norman Foster [or Norman Robert Foster, Baron Foster of Thames Bank]
Jony Ive [accept Sir Jonathan Paul Ive]
